Free Text: There is a badger sett on the down side. It is illegal to capture, injure or kill badgers in their sett, to damage, destroy or obstruct access to a sett. Guidelines on the permitted working distances from badger setts should be followed: Using very heavy machinery within 30 metres of any entrance to an active sett, or lighter machinery i.e. for any digging operation, within 20 metres, or light work such as digging with hand tools or scrub clearance within 10 metres. All activities require a licence. All staff to be made aware via tool box talks. Further information can be found using the Biodiversity Action Plan - BAP.

ELR Name: NEWCASTLE MLGE CHGE - CARLISLE S JN
Free Text: 4no. UTX's crossing the UP and Down main lines, with minimum 650mm clearance from underside of sleeper to avoid being in the track renewal bed have been installed to tie an existing retaining sheet pile wall on the up to 4no. new structures (sheet pile retaining walls) on the down side of the track to strengthen the existing structure. All sheet piles are buried, marker plates are installed on the sleepers for locations of the crossings that extend to the retaining structures in the cess. There's sign posts in the cess to help identify start and finish of the sheet pile structures by being at the same offset and starting and finishing at the extremities of the structures.

ELR Name: NEWCASTLE MLGE CHGE - CARLISLE S JN
Free Text:
Hazard present for future excavation works to renew / refurbish existing drainage within the Down cess. The design of the sheet pile / tie bar system installed here does not allow for excavation on the rail side of the Down cess sheets when the system is fully activated (i.e. when 4.5m of  retention is lost on the embankment side of the Up cess sheets).  Prior to any future drainage renewal work, it should first be checked that the Up side embankment profile has not changed significantly and the retention mentioned above is present.
